settings_quality_formatsDescription: Custom formats define matching rules for releases. Create formats to match specific codecs, audio formats, release groups, and more. Use conditions to precisely target the releases you want.
settings_quality_profilesDescription: Quality profiles determine how releases are scored. Each profile assigns scores to different formats (resolution, audio, HDR, etc.) to rank releases by quality.
settings_quality_subtitle: Manage quality profiles and custom formats to control how releases are scored and selected.
settings_streaming_cacheDefaultsNotice: Cache settings are currently using defaults. Custom configuration coming soon.
settings_streaming_extractionCacheDescri: Compressed Usenet releases are downloaded and extracted locally for streaming. Extracted files are cached to enable instant playback on subsequent views.
settings_streaming_retentionHint: Extracted files are automatically deleted after this period
settings_streaming_stepCleanupDesc: Extracted files are automatically removed after the retention period to save disk space.
settings_streaming_stepDetectionDesc: When you select a Usenet release for streaming, the system checks if the content is compressed (RAR, 7z, ZIP).
settings_streaming_stepDownloadDesc: Compressed content is downloaded from your NNTP servers with parallel segment fetching for maximum speed.
settings_streaming_stepExtractionDesc: The archive is extracted to your root folder's extraction cache directory.
settings_streaming_stepStreamingDesc: Once extracted, the media file is available for instant playback with full seeking support.
settings_subtitles_noSubtitlesAvailable: No downloaded subtitle files are available for sync yet.
settings_subtitles_offsetOnlyDesc: Apply a constant time shift without introducing splits. Much faster.
settings_subtitles_tryAdjustingFilters: Try adjusting your language profile or search filters
settings_system_apiAuthDescription: API keys allow external applications to access Cinephage programmatically. Use the Main key for full access, or the Media Streaming key for media server integration with Live TV and streaming content.
settings_system_backup_confirmMessage: This will apply {count} selected backup section(s) to the current instance. This action cannot be undone.
settings_system_backup_cookieWarning: This backup includes encrypted indexer session cookies. Restoring it will try to reuse active indexer sessions where they are still valid.
settings_system_backup_errorMissingData: Backup file is missing configuration data
settings_system_backup_errorPassphraseRe: Backup passphrase is required for restore.
settings_system_backup_errorSelectSectio: Select at least one backup section to restore.
settings_system_backup_exportDescription: Exports configuration for settings, integrations, indexers, download clients, profiles, and other user-managed setup required to rebuild the instance.
settings_system_backup_exportPassphraseP: At least 16 characters to encrypt exported secrets
settings_system_backup_includeCookiesHel: Back up active 2FA/session cookies for indexers that rely on them. These are encrypted, short-lived, and more sensitive than saved credentials.
settings_system_backup_noticeDescription: Backups include user-managed settings and encrypted user-supplied credentials. Runtime caches, queues, task history, and generated system API keys are excluded. Indexer session cookies can be included explicitly as an advanced option.
settings_system_backup_restoreDescriptio: Restoring applies the saved configuration from the backup file to this instance. Use the same passphrase that was used during export.
settings_system_backup_subtitle: Export your Cinephage configuration for disaster recovery and restore it later with the same passphrase.
settings_system_exampleEmpty: Leave empty for direct HTTP access
settings_system_externalUrlDescription: Set the public-facing URL for your Cinephage instance. This is used for authentication callbacks and generating correct links when behind a reverse proxy.
settings_system_general_subtitle: Manage API keys and external URL settings.
settings_system_invalidUrlFormat: Please enter a valid URL (e.g., https://cinephage.example.com)
settings_system_mediaStreamingDescriptio: Use this key with media servers like Jellyfin, Plex, or Emby for M3U playlist, EPG access, and streaming content (.strm files).
settings_system_metadataProviders_subtit: Connect and manage metadata sources for titles, artwork, and ratings.
settings_system_noApiKeys: No API keys found. Generate keys to enable external access.
settings_system_noKeyToRegenerate: No {label} API key found to regenerate
settings_system_publicUrlHint: If you access Cinephage through a reverse proxy with HTTPS, enter your public URL here (e.g., https://cinephage.yourdomain.com). Leave empty for local HTTP access.
settings_system_regenerateApiKeyMessage: Are you sure you want to regenerate this API key? The current key will be permanently destroyed and any applications using it will stop working immediately.
settings_system_subtitle: Configure system-level settings including API authentication.
settings_tasks_customTaskDescription: Custom task creation will allow you to:
settings_tasks_featureFilterByMedia: Filter tasks by media type, quality, or tags
settings_tasks_noHistoryAvailable: No history available for this task.
settings_tasks_subtitle: Manage scheduled and maintenance tasks for your Cinephage instance

sizeLimitsHint: For season packs, the average size per episode is calculated.

smartlists_external_importListsDescripti: Movies and TV shows from the source will be displayed together. The media type selector at the top only affects TMDB Discover lists.

smartlists_external_jsonUrlHint: Enter the URL to a JSON file containing Movie/TV show data.
smartlists_info_mixedContentDescription: Movies and TV shows from the source will be displayed together. The media type selector at the top only affects TMDB Discover lists.

status_tooltipHealthy: Download client is healthy and reachable
streamingProfileInfo: Streaming profiles use external sources, so size limits are not configurable.

task_createModal_description: Custom task creation will allow you to:
task_createModal_feature_filters: Filter tasks by media type, quality, or tags
task_desc_cutoffUnmet: Search for releases for content below the quality profile cutoff
task_desc_historyCleanup: Automatically purge old history entries based on the retention period setting
task_desc_libraryScan: Scan root folders for media files. Run this after adding folders or if media is missing.

task_desc_missingSubtitles: Download subtitles for media files missing required languages
task_desc_newEpisode: Search for recently aired episodes of monitored series
task_desc_pendingRelease: Process releases waiting in the delay profile queue
task_desc_reprobeStrmMediaInfo: Reprobe existing .strm files to refresh media info, excluding Streamer Profile .strm files.
task_desc_smartListRefresh: Check and update smart lists based on their configured refresh intervals
task_desc_subtitleUpgrade: Search for better-scoring subtitles to replace existing ones
task_desc_updateStrmUrls: Update all .strm files with the current streaming base URL. Run this after changing your server address, port, or domain.
task_desc_upgrade: Search for better quality releases to replace existing files
